sections.title: Stations (right to left, following salmon flow):
items: 🔴 Corte (4p) – Initial cutting and defect removal
🟡 Espinas (6p) – Bone removal
🟢 Supervision (1p) – Ensures salmon enters the skinning machine correctly
🔵 Acabado Corte (2p) – Final cutting
🟣 Trim (2p) – Trimming to specification
🔵 Cajas (1p) – Boxing
🟣 Etiquetador (1p) – Labelling

sections.text: The cutting station is the first position on the production line. 4 workers (2 per side) process each salmon fillet as it arrives.

sections.title: Position 1 – Right side operator:
items: 🔪 Cuts the lower part and fat of the salmon in a half-moon shape
👀 Removes the main defects and imperfections from the fillet
📋 Must follow the trim specification of the current order

sections.title: Position 2 – Left side operator:
items: 🔪 Cuts the entire white lateral strip from the fillet
👀 Ensures a clean, uniform edge
📋 Must follow the trim specification of the current order

sections.text: You will be informed at all times which Trim class production is running (A Trim to E Trim). Always follow the current specification!

steps.label: Trim B: + Backfin, collar, belly flap, tail off
Trim D: Fully trimmed, membrane off

sections.text: Always handle knives carefully. Cut away from your body. Change blue gloves immediately if torn.
The bone removal station is one of the busiest positions. 6 workers (3 per side, mirrored) remove pin bones from the salmon fillets.

sections.title: Before you start – The BAADER 988 machine:
items: ⚙️ Before the fillets reach you, they pass through the BAADER 988 machine
🦴 This machine automatically removes most of the pin bones
🔧 Your job is to find and remove the remaining bones using pliers
🌐 See the machine in action: https://www.baader.com/product/baader-988

sections.title: How the fillets are distributed:
items: 🐟 Three salmon loins arrive on the belt at the same time
1️⃣ Bottom loin → Position 1 (closest to the belt)
2️⃣ Middle loin → Position 2 (center)
3️⃣ Top loin → Position 3 (furthest from the belt)

sections.title: How to remove bones – step by step:

animatedSteps.title: Pick up the fillet
animatedSteps.description: Take your assigned loin from the belt (bottom, middle, or top)

animatedSteps.title: Check the head area
animatedSteps.description: The head area ALWAYS contains more than 3 pin bones. Find and remove them all

animatedSteps.title: Slide your hand along the loin
animatedSteps.description: Run your hand along the entire length of the fillet to feel for remaining bones

animatedSteps.title: Remove all bones found
animatedSteps.description: Pull out each bone you feel with your tool. Be thorough – no bones should remain

animatedSteps.title: Verify and release
animatedSteps.description: Only when you are sure there are no more bones, place the fillet back on the conveyor belt

animatedSteps.title: Next fillet
animatedSteps.description: Immediately start with the next incoming fillet

sections.text: NEVER rush this step. A bone left in the fillet is a quality failure and a risk for the consumer. Take the time needed to check thoroughly.

sections.title: Key reminders:
items: ⚠️ The head area always has the most bones – check it carefully first
✋ Use your hand to feel the entire loin – bones can hide anywhere
🔄 Work at a steady pace – speed comes with practice
🩹 If you cut yourself, stop immediately and contact a team leader (green)

sections.text: This position has 1 worker. Your job is simple but critical: ensure every fillet enters the skinning machine in the correct orientation.

sections.title: What you must do:
items: 🐟 Position every fillet with the tail facing the direction of the conveyor belt
↔️ Fillets must go straight – they cannot enter sideways or at an angle
⚙️ If a fillet enters crooked, the skinning machine will jam
🔄 Work continuously – every fillet must be checked and aligned

sections.text: A jammed machine stops the entire line. Always make sure the tail points in the direction of travel and the fillet is straight.
This is one of the most demanding positions. 2 workers (1 per side). Skinless fillets arrive from the machine and must be cleaned, trimmed and flipped with speed and precision.

animatedSteps.title: Receive the fillet
animatedSteps.description: Skinless fillets drop from the conveyor after the skinning machine

animatedSteps.title: Remove imperfections
animatedSteps.description: Cut away any remaining skin, dark spots, bruises or damaged areas

animatedSteps.title: Remove excess fat
animatedSteps.description: Trim any remaining fat that shouldn't be on the final product

animatedSteps.title: Flip the fillet
animatedSteps.description: Turn the fillet over so it continues presentation-side up on the belt

animatedSteps.title: Release or reject
animatedSteps.description: If the fillet looks good, place it back on the belt. If it's beyond repair, put it in the side boxes

sections.title: Side boxes – rejected fillets:
items: 📦 Fillets that are too damaged to sell as premium go into the side boxes
♻️ These are NOT wasted – they are reused in other parts of the plant
⚠️ Only reject a fillet if it truly cannot be repaired with trimming

sections.text: This position requires excellent knife skills and speed. Always cut away from your body. Precision is key – too much trimming wastes product, too little fails quality.
The Trim position requires skill and practice. 2 workers (1 per side). You operate a circular cutting machine that cuts along the central line of the fillet.

sections.title: How to use the trim machine:

animatedSteps.title: Position the fillet
animatedSteps.description: Place the fillet on the machine platform with the head end on your left

animatedSteps.title: Tilt the machine ~30°
animatedSteps.description: Keep the machine tilted at about 30 degrees – don't press it flat down. This is the most efficient angle

animatedSteps.title: Cut left to right
animatedSteps.description: Guide from head to tail (left to right). There's no written rule, but this direction is easier and gives a cleaner cut

animatedSteps.title: Don't push the machine down
animatedSteps.description: The key technique: DON'T sink the machine into the fillet. Keep it light and at an angle for a smooth cut

animatedSteps.title: Trimmings to the metal tub
animatedSteps.description: The cut-off strip goes into the metal tub at your side – keep it clean and organized

sections.title: Key tips:
items: 📐 ~30° angle is more efficient than pressing flat
➡️ Head to tail (left to right) gives the best result
🪶 Light touch – don't force the machine down into the flesh
🪣 Trimmings always go in the metal tub beside you
🔄 Practice makes perfect – speed comes with experience

sections.text: The circular blade is extremely sharp. Keep your hands clear of the cutting area at all times. Focus and concentration are essential.

sections.title: Stations (right to left, following salmon flow):
items: 🔴 Cutting (4p) – Initial cutting and defect removal
🟡 Bones (6p) – Bone removal
🟢 Perfection (1p) – Quality control and final touch-up
🔵 Box (2p) – Packing into boxes
🟣 Weight (1p) – Weight control
🟣 Not Approved (1p) – Sorting non-approved products

👀 Removes the main defects and imperfections from the fillet

steps.label: Trim B: + Backfin, collar, belly flap, tail off
Trim D: Fully trimmed, membrane off

animatedSteps.description: Take your assigned loin from the belt (bottom, middle, or top)

animatedSteps.title: Check the head area
animatedSteps.description: The head area ALWAYS contains more than 3 pin bones. Find and remove them all

animatedSteps.title: Slide your hand along the loin

🩹 If you cut yourself, stop immediately and contact a team leader (green)

sections.text: This position has 1 worker. Your job is to inspect each fillet and remove all imperfections with a knife.

sections.title: How to work – step by step:

animatedSteps.title: Receive the fillet
animatedSteps.description: Take the fillet arriving from the previous station

animatedSteps.title: Clean with knife
animatedSteps.description: Remove all imperfections from the fillet using your knife

animatedSteps.title: Approved fillet
animatedSteps.description: Place the fillet PERPENDICULAR to the conveyor belt – the next colleague will pick it up

animatedSteps.title: Not approved fillet
animatedSteps.description: Place the fillet PARALLEL to the conveyor belt – it goes to sorting

sections.text: The direction you place the fillet is critical: PERPENDICULAR = approved (colleague picks it up). PARALLEL = not approved (goes to sorting).
2 workers at this position. They work as a team: one packs, one ensures supplies.

sections.title: Person 1 – Packer (on the line):
items: 🐟 Picks approved fillets from the belt (those placed perpendicular)
📦 Places fillets in the box according to the current order
⚖️ Follows order specification for quantity and weight
📋 When the box is full, pushes it to the weighing station

sections.title: Person 2 – Box supplier:
items: 📦 Ensures there are ALWAYS empty boxes available for the packer
🔄 Replaces full boxes with empty ones immediately
⚠️ The packer should never have to wait for an empty box – keep up the pace

sections.text: The box supplier is key to keeping the line running. A packer without boxes stops all production.
1 worker at this position. You are the last step before the box leaves the line.

sections.title: How to work – step by step:

animatedSteps.title: Collect the box
animatedSteps.description: Take the full box from the packing station

animatedSteps.title: Weigh the box
animatedSteps.description: Place the box on the scale and verify the weight matches the order

animatedSteps.title: Label
animatedSteps.description: If the weight is correct, print and attach the label to the box

animatedSteps.title: Send forward
animatedSteps.description: Place the labelled box on the conveyor belt for shipping

sections.text: Never label a box that hasn't reached the correct weight. The weight is determined by the order specification.

sections.title: Portions
sections.description: Portioning, vacuum packaging and skins area

sections.title: Portions Area – Click an area to see instructions
items: Vacuum 1
Vacuum 2
Portioning 1
Packaging
Portioning 2
Skins
Click an area to see instructions ↓

sections.text: If there is no fish (common first thing in the morning), there is always work to do. You need to prepare the boxes that will be used during the day. It is impossible to have nothing to do in portions. Be proactive and stay ahead of problems.

sections.title: Work areas:
items: 🔴 Vacuum 1 (6p) – Vacuum packing whole fillets: tray, feeders, collector, weigher, loader
🟢 Vacuum 2 (3p) – Vacuum packing portions: feeder, collector, weigher
🔵 Portioning 1 (3p) – Portion cutting machine: feeder, selector, helper
🟣 Packaging (1p) – Collects metal containers and packages by type
🔵 Portioning 2 (4p) – Second portioning machine: feeder, collector, assistant, carrier
🟣 Skins (1p) – Separating skins and meat into 14kg boxes

sections.text: This area has 6 workers and is responsible for vacuum packaging whole salmon fillets.

animatedSteps.title: Tray
animatedSteps.description: Must feed the vacuum machine with trays for the fish.

animatedSteps.title: Feeder 1
animatedSteps.description: Places fish into the vacuum packaging machine.

animatedSteps.title: Feeder 2
animatedSteps.description: Places fish into the vacuum packaging machine.

animatedSteps.title: Collector
animatedSteps.description: Collects the vacuum-packed fish and places it on the scale for the colleague.

animatedSteps.title: Weigher
animatedSteps.description: This person must weigh it and put a label on it.

animatedSteps.title: Loader
animatedSteps.description: Fills boxes and places them on the pallet.

sections.text: This area has 3 workers and is responsible for vacuum packaging pre-cut salmon pieces.

animatedSteps.title: Portion feeder
animatedSteps.description: This person puts the pre-cut salmon pieces into the machine.

animatedSteps.title: Collector
animatedSteps.description: Collects the vacuum-packed pieces and puts them in boxes.

animatedSteps.title: Weigher
animatedSteps.description: This person weighs, labels and sends it to the conveyor belt.

sections.text: This area has 3 workers and is responsible for cutting salmon fillets into portions.

animatedSteps.title: Feeder
animatedSteps.description: This person puts the salmon on the belt, may need to cut part of the fillet to fit the size.

animatedSteps.title: Selector
animatedSteps.description: This person selects the good pieces and puts them in boxes.

animatedSteps.title: Helper (optional)
animatedSteps.description: This person is optional and only present when needed for plastics or finishing boxes.

sections.title: Work position:
items: 📦 Packager (1p) – Person responsible for collecting all metal containers and packaging by type into their boxes and placing them on the belt.

sections.text: This area has 4 workers and is the second portioning line.

animatedSteps.title: Feeder
animatedSteps.description: This person feeds fish into the machine at a constant pace and ensures supply doesn't run out.

animatedSteps.title: Collector
animatedSteps.description: A position requiring great manual dexterity and effectiveness in processing what is good or bad.

animatedSteps.title: Assistant
animatedSteps.description: This person places intermediate plastics in the boxes.

animatedSteps.title: Carrier
animatedSteps.description: Person responsible for placing the box on the scale, weighing and passing to the production line.

sections.text: This area has 1 worker responsible for separating skins and meat into 14kg boxes.

sections.title: Position instructions:
items: 🐟 Skins go in boxes WITHOUT blue bags and with a yellow label lid
🥩 Meat goes in boxes WITH plastic and with a white label lid
📋 If you run out of labels, ask the portions team leader

sections.text: 3 workers at this station. Work is divided into two zones: cork boxes and cardboard boxes. Lines have extremely high box demand – keep up the pace!

sections.title: Zone layout:
items: 🟤 Cork boxes (2 people) – Large and small cork boxes
📦 Cardboard boxes (1 person) – Belly boxes (small), Backbone/Head boxes (large), Portion boxes (skin & meat)

sections.text: Most important: distribution of LARGE boxes must never stop. Backbone and Head generate the highest demand.
The work is simple but must be VERY consistent. Demand from the lines is extremely high.

sections.title: Your tasks:
items: 📦 Assemble cork boxes – large and small – at a steady pace
🔄 Keep the ramp continuously filled with boxes
⚡ The lines never stop – you must keep up with demand

sections.text: IMPORTANT: Check REPEATEDLY that boxes are actually sliding down the ramp! They often get stuck, giving you a false sense that the line doesn't need boxes. Check the ramp constantly!

sections.title: Common trap:
items: ⚠️ Boxes get stuck on the ramp → you think demand is low
🔍 Check the ramp regularly – push stuck boxes through
🚨 If the line runs out of boxes, all production stops

sections.text: This position requires more skill. You feed two lines with boxes and must handle several types simultaneously.

sections.title: 1. Belly boxes (SMALL):
items: 📦 Small cardboard boxes for Belly production
🔵 Must include plastic cover
🆕 With new machine (being implemented): must also include lid

sections.title: 2. Backbone/Head boxes (LARGE):
items: 📦 Large cardboard boxes for Backbone and Head
🔵 Must include plastic cover
🆕 With new machine (being implemented): must also include lid
🚨 HIGHEST PRIORITY – Backbone and Head generate most demand

sections.title: 3. Portion boxes – Skin & Meat:
items: 📦 Brown on the outside, white on the inside
🔧 Just assemble them and send down the ramp
✅ The simplest box type

sections.text: ALWAYS prioritize large boxes (Backbone/Head). They have the highest demand due to high production rate. Never let large boxes run out!
2 workers at this position. You work together all day. One adds ice and jelly, the other puts on lids.

sections.title: Person 1 – Ice & Jelly:
items: 🧊 Adds ice to the boxes to maintain temperature
💊 Adds jelly (plastic pads that help with temperature control) to the boxes
🔄 Continuously replenishes ice and jelly – never let it run out

sections.title: Person 2 – Lids:
items: 📦 Puts lids on boxes – either cork or cardboard
🔄 Always ensures enough lids are available
⚠️ Check that the lid type matches the order

sections.text: You are responsible for always replenishing what gets used up – ice, jelly and lids. Never run out of materials.
Belly is a highly valued cut, especially in the Asian market. It is a narrow, elongated part of the salmon.

animatedSteps.title: Receive the belly flaps
animatedSteps.description: Belly flaps arrive from the production line – narrow, elongated pieces

animatedSteps.title: Pack in 17 kg boxes
animatedSteps.description: Place the belly flaps in boxes that should weigh 17 kg

animatedSteps.title: Plastic separator at ~10 kg
animatedSteps.description: When the box reaches about 10 kg, insert a plastic separator before continuing to fill

animatedSteps.title: Print label
animatedSteps.description: Use the shared printer to print the label

animatedSteps.title: Watch the label
animatedSteps.description: You share the printer with your colleague – pay attention so the label doesn't fall on the floor

sections.text: You use the SAME printer. Pay attention and make sure the label doesn't fall on the floor. Coordinate with your colleague.
This is the most demanding position. Fish backbones fall into the bin and you must fill boxes with 18 kg. About 45 boxes in 30 minutes – keep the pace!

sections.title: Person 1 – Filler:
items: 🦴 Takes fish backbones from the bin
📦 Fills boxes to 18 kg
⚡ Works fast – about 45 boxes per 30-minute session

sections.title: Person 2 – Preparer:
items: 📦 Prepares empty boxes and lids
🔄 Ensures the filler never waits for materials
⚖️ Helps check weight and close boxes

sections.text: Pace is key. 45 boxes in 30 minutes means under 40 seconds per box. Teamwork is essential.
This is perhaps the calmest stage and the only time you separate from your partner.

sections.title: Person 1 – Heads (usually the one NOT doing backbone):
items: 🐟 Places salmon heads into 18 kg boxes
📦 Boxes go WITHOUT lids
⚖️ Make sure the weight is correct

sections.title: Person 2 – Support & Cleaning:
items: 🧹 Cleans and tidies the line as much as possible
🗑️ Empties the skin offcut tank
📦 Helps other stations with lids and materials
🧼 Cleans the floor and solves problems for colleagues

sections.text: If you're not on heads: your job is to support everyone else. Cleaning, restocking materials and problem-solving.
